text:
My perspective feels slightly askew, both literally and philosophically. In the first scene, I stand outside a reflective glass wall, peering into an ordered, empty meeting room. It is sterile and quiet; the environment signals anticipation, as though waiting for people and ideas to fill it. Yet, there is a touch of alienation—am I truly part of this space, or always observing from the threshold?

In the second scene, I am enveloped by the vibrant, dynamic chaos of creativity. The "R+D" wall pulses with energy, colored by the promise of innovation and collaboration. The written words and video reflections nearby amplify my sense of wonder and curiosity. Here, I feel excitement, a kind of purposeful disorientation, as my thoughts dance around the possibility of invention. I am reminded of the importance of anticipation—how it both unsettles and motivates, fueling the pursuit of new meaning.
